div.h2: Michael-Scott Lock-Free Queue: Two-Step CAS Protocol with Helping

ul.li: Queue has elements A, B, C
System is quiescent, ready for new enqueue

div.p: Invariant Check: tail->next == NULL Protocol: Proceed to Phase 1 (Linking)

ul.li: Thread T1 linked node D successfully
tail still points to C (Advanced pointer pending)
State is inconsistent but logically valid

div.p: CAS 1 Succeeded: C->next: NULL → D CAS 2 Pending: tail: C → D Threads seeing this state must "Help"

ul.li: Thread T2 attempts to enqueue E
T2 observes the lagging tail at C
T2 performs CAS(&q->tail, C, D) to help T1
This ensures the data structure never deadlocks

div.p: T2 helps T1: CAS(tail, C, D) Advances tail pointer so T2 can eventually link node E

ul.li: The tail pointer is now synchronized with data
Node D is now a full member of the queue

div.p: Quiescent State restored. Either T1 finished or T2 helped. Next enqueue can now begin.
